name: Offshore Reinsurer Cold Call (Cayman / Bermuda Direct)
description: Short, direct script for Cayman/Bermuda reinsurers. 10 nodes. Peer-to-peer tone — no education layer, no Lloyd's formality. These markets know RV. Goal: confirm fit, pitch the structure, and get the underwriting package in their hands. Core message: proven RV structure, new asset class, $3.465B ceded limit, going direct.

nodes.label: Right person?
nodes.script: Hi, this is [caller] from Corgi Insurance Services. I'm looking for whoever handles new RV or equipment program submissions — is that you?

nodes.label: Direct program pitch
nodes.script: We're building a reinsurance tower for a GPU Residual Value Guaranty program — $3.465B of ceded limit. The structure is auto RV applied to data-center GPUs: put-style floor on hardware value at expiry, SiliconNavigator™ benchmarks the value test, title transfer at settlement eliminates moral hazard. Per-cohort occurrence basis, tranched rollout. Issuing carrier is DIAIC, program manager is Corgi. We've raised $108M. We're going direct to Cayman and Bermuda because the structure is clean and you don't need an intermediary to evaluate it. Are you looking at new RV programs?

branches.condition: what's the underlying data / pricing basis
how big is the program / capacity targets

nodes.label: Bad time
nodes.script: No problem. We're building a reinsurance tower for a GPU RV program — $3.465B of ceded limit — and going direct to Cayman and Bermuda for capacity. Worth 15 minutes when you have it. When works?

nodes.label: Program structure
nodes.script: The structure: GPU hardware depreciates at ~20% per year. We write a put-style floor — 5%, 10%, or 15% of original purchase price — over 3-, 5-, or 10-year terms. At expiry, SiliconNavigator™ benchmarks the market value. If it's below the floor, we pay the guaranteed amount and take title to the hardware. Title transfer eliminates moral hazard completely. Per-cohort occurrence means each hardware batch is a discrete event — no cross-site accumulation. Monte Carlo-modelled across normal depreciation and generation-cycle shock scenarios. Want the actuarial package?

nodes.label: SiliconNavigator pricing data
nodes.script: SiliconNavigator™ is the secondary market benchmark for H100 and A100 GPUs — 80%+ market coverage, daily pricing, 3+ years of history. It's the index we use for the value test at expiry. Fully independent, auditable, transparent — your team verifies the loss calculation without relying on our numbers. No black-box valuation.

nodes.label: Program size and capacity
nodes.script: We're targeting $3.465B of total ceded limit across the full tower, built in tranched layers — no single reinsurer takes the whole book. We're looking for quota share partners at each layer. What does {company}'s appetite look like for a new RV program in the tech/equipment space?

branches.condition: need to see the actuarial package first

nodes.label: Carrier and program manager
nodes.script: Issuing carrier is DIAIC — Digital Infrastructure Assurance Insurance Company, Utah-domiciled captive. Corgi Insurance Services is the program manager. We've raised $108M to capitalize the program. Full financials are in the underwriting package.

branches.condition: tell me more about the structure

nodes.label: Send underwriting package
nodes.script: I'll get you the full package today — actuarial model, Monte Carlo loss distributions, SiliconNavigator pricing methodology, treaty structure, DIAIC financials. Who's the right person to receive it, and when would be a good time for a 20-minute call to walk through it?

branches.condition: just send it / we'll review first

nodes.label: Wrong person
nodes.script: No problem. Who at {company} handles new RV or equipment program submissions?

nodes.label: Exit
nodes.script: Understood. If GPU RV capacity becomes relevant for {company}, Corgi Insurance Services — worth a call. Thanks.

no_gpu_history.trigger: no historical data / too new / no loss history
no_gpu_history.response: The data we have is SiliconNavigator — 3+ years of daily secondary market prices, 80%+ H100/A100 coverage. Monte Carlo modelled across normal depreciation and generation-cycle shock. Title transfer eliminates moral hazard structurally. The actuarial package is built to reinsurance standard. Want me to send it?

systemic_risk.trigger: systemic / what if GPU market crashes / correlated losses
systemic_risk.response: Per-cohort occurrence means no cross-site sweep. A market-wide correction produces multiple cohort claims bounded by the book written in that treaty year — same as any equipment RV down cycle. The Monte Carlo models a 30-50% market-wide shock scenario explicitly. It's in the actuarial package.

moral_hazard.trigger: moral hazard / what stops people from filing false claims
moral_hazard.response: Title transfer at settlement. To collect, the insured surrenders the hardware. You can't claim on hardware you're still using. Real economic constraint — structurally eliminates the incentive.

exitStates.BOOK_MEETING: Log meeting, send calendar invite, send full underwriting package same day
exitStates.SEND_PACKAGE_THEN_FOLLOWUP: Send actuarial package + DIAIC financials today, follow up in 3 days
